scenario: Catalyst Brokerage runs 8000 instances per day in Camunda 8.8 and a developer needs an idiomatic FEEL one-liner to compute a customer's current age in years from a birthday variable inside a DMN decision input column on the eligibility decision.
question: Which FEEL expression correctly returns the integer age in years from a birthday date according to the temporal examples page?

options.text: date(birthday) - today() since FEEL subtraction of two date values yields a years-months-duration whose value is the integer age at evaluation time
round half up(today() - birthday) since FEEL implicitly converts the days-time-duration result into a number of years at evaluation time
years and months duration(today() - birthday) since the duration subtraction already returns a years-months-duration value to read directly
years and months duration(date(birthday), today()).years which returns the normalized years component of the resulting years-months-duration value

a.text: Incorrect. Subtracting two date values yields a days-time-duration, not a years-months-duration; the example uses the years and months duration function to obtain a years-months-duration value.

b.text: Incorrect. FEEL does not implicitly convert a days-time-duration into years; the example explicitly uses years and months duration to obtain the years-months-duration and then reads its years property.

c.text: Incorrect. today() - birthday returns a days-time-duration, not a years-months-duration; the years and months duration function with two arguments is required to obtain the years-months result.

d.text: Correct. The Calculate age example uses years and months duration(date(birthday), today()).years to return the current age based on the given birthday and today's date.

explanation: The Calculate age example on the temporal expressions page documents years and months duration(date(birthday), today()).years as the canonical FEEL expression for current age in completed years. It builds a years-months-duration between the two dates and reads its years component.
